title: spergula arvensis subsp. linicola
intro: Spergula arvensis subsp. linicola, commonly known as Spurge Laurel, is a subspecies of the Corn Spurrey plant, belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Spergula arvensis subsp. linicola include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues.
